what_it_does: This file is a session handoff document that records what was built, committed, verified, and left unverified during the 2026-06-07 model/VRAM layer, voice co-residence, persona identity, and cognition-planning session; it also marks merge-conflict territory and scopes the next Concurrent Cognition loop.
observations: An enumerated table of 8 commits and what each delivered.
Current live state is chat-4b at 64K / util 0.49 co-resident with 4-bit AWQ Orpheus at util 0.32.
A deliberately blunt verification-status section split into proven-by-use, code-complete-but-not-verified, and known-unknown/not-done buckets.
The model/VRAM layer makes each service's config block the single source for both budget gate and launch.
Persona identity bug fixed so the persona's brain prose reaches the prompt, plus any-persona x any-engine voice mapping.
qwen3tts and cosyvoice measured as fixed-footprint engines using vram_mb rather than gpu_util.
Voice co-residence mechanism and proof: 4-bit AWQ Orpheus is the enabler on a 16GB card.
Click-to-indicate gated behind a deliberate point mode to fix settings close and translucency bugs.
Detailed merge-conflict territory list with HOT FILE labels and touched regions.
Concurrent Cognition next-loop landscape, idea, territory, and two pending Tim soul-forks.
Open/pending/needs-Tim items and drive/verify commands at the end.
standouts: The unusually candid three-bucket verification status that explicitly says much is unverified or not done.
Proof that a 4-bit AWQ Orpheus co-resides with a 64K-context 4B brain on a 16GB card.
The explicit merge-conflict territory list tied to specific files and line regions.
The canonical disclaimer: STATE.md + tests win; fix this handoff if it disagrees.
Pre-scoping of the upcoming Concurrent Cognition loop to prevent merge collisions.
conventions: Follows the project handoff format with read order, status: living, tags, and numbered sections.
Uses tables for commits and verification status.
Labels files as HOT FILE when they are likely merge-conflict territory.
References external canonical docs and memory links like [[project-concurrent-cognition]].
Mixes prose, inline code commands, and endpoint examples.
concerns: Much of the verification evidence is the author's own reported use rather than reproducible artifacts inside the file.
A long list of code-complete-but-unverified items could be mistaken for working if the verification section is skipped.
The merge-conflict territory may become stale as other sessions continue changing the same hot files.
Headline on-device voice UX is unproven despite server-side pieces being proven, which could be overstated if skimmed.
notes: A candid post-session handoff that documents committed model/VRAM/voice changes, separates proven from unverified, flags hot merge files, and scopes the upcoming Concurrent Cognition build.
questions: What exactly defines a 'part' in the planned Concurrent Cognition staging?
Which 32 roles will form the first swarm cast beyond the judge?
Where is the raw measured footprint data stored, since only summaries appear here?
How is the 16GB VRAM ceiling and WSL baseline overhead configured?
What specific content from the previous 2026-06-06 voice-live handoff is prerequisite beyond the stated read order?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches its stated purpose and adds a self-correcting canonical disclaimer plus an unusually explicit verification-status section.
apparent_themes: Honest epistemic status: proven vs unverified vs not done.
Co-residence of brain and voice as a non-negotiable constraint.
Config block as single source of truth for sizing and launch.
Merge-aware territory marking for parallel sessions.
Staged concurrent cognition as the next architectural direction.
intention_signals: It tries to prevent downstream sessions from assuming code-complete means working, to protect ongoing merge work by marking hot files, and to pre-allocate territory for the upcoming Concurrent Cognition loop so parallel work does not collide.
novelty: The explicit three-bucket verification-status section, the soul-forks framing for pending design decisions, and the detailed commit-to-file merge-conflict territory map are distinctive.
contribution: This file contributes a candid, territory-aware handoff that records not just what changed but what is proven, unverified, and likely to conflict, plus a scoped research landscape for the next feature loop; a fused system would keep its verification-status discipline and merge-territory warnings.
summary_for_embedding: A living handoff document recording the 2026-06-07 session's model/VRAM layer, voice co-residence proof, persona identity hookup, unverified items, merge-conflict hot files, and the planned Concurrent Cognition loop.
intention_for_embedding: To orient fresh agents and parallel sessions to the current committed state, honest verification gaps, and upcoming cognition build territory.
